topics.name: 100 Level: Introduction to Mass Communication and Media Writing
topics.description: MAC 101 Introduction to Mass Communication, MAC 111 Media Writing, ENG 101 Use of English.

subtopics.name: MAC 101: Theories and Functions of Mass Communication
objectives: Define mass communication and identify its key functions: informing, educating, entertaining, and persuading
Explain major communication models — Shannon-Weaver, Berlo, Osgood-Schramm — and their limitations

subtopics.name: MAC 111: News Writing and Editing
objectives: Write news stories using the inverted pyramid structure with proper attribution and objectivity
Apply AP and NUJ style guide rules in editing copy for print and online platforms

topics.name: 200–300 Level: Broadcast, Print Journalism, Advertising, PR, and Media Law
topics.description: MAC 201 Broadcast Journalism, MAC 211 Advertising, MAC 221 Public Relations, MAC 301 Media Law & Ethics.

subtopics.name: MAC 201 & MAC 211: Broadcast Production and Advertising Strategy
objectives: Produce radio and television news packages, features, and programmes using broadcast equipment
Develop advertising campaigns including target audience analysis, creative brief, and media plan

subtopics.name: MAC 301: Nigerian Media Law, Ethics, and Freedom of the Press
objectives: Explain defamation law, right to privacy, copyright, and contempt of court as they apply to journalists
Analyse ethical frameworks — objectivity, truth, fairness, minimising harm — using Nigerian media case studies

topics.name: 400 Level: Digital Media, Research Methods, and Professional Project
topics.description: MAC 401 Digital Media & Online Journalism, MAC 411 Research Methods, MAC 499 Final Year Project.

subtopics.name: MAC 401: Online Journalism, Social Media, and Data Journalism
objectives: Produce multimedia news packages integrating video, audio, text, and infographics for online platforms
Apply data journalism techniques using spreadsheets and visualisation tools for investigative reporting

subtopics.name: MAC 499: Mass Communication Research Project
objectives: Conduct a scholarly communication study using quantitative (survey) or qualitative (interview/content analysis) methods
Present findings in research report format and defend before UNILAG Social Sciences Faculty board
